title: Link scheduling — scheduled_from and scheduled_until support
description: Implement link scheduling: links in links.yaml can have optional scheduled_from and/or scheduled_until date fields. The static generator evaluates scheduling at build time and only includes links that are currently active. The `links list` command shows scheduling status.

features: Implement src/utils.js: isLinkActive(link) — returns true if the link should be included in the current build based on today's date vs scheduled_from and scheduled_until. Rules: no dates = always active; only scheduled_from = active from that date onwards; only scheduled_until = active until that date; both = active within the window
Update src/generator.js to call isLinkActive(link) and filter out inactive links before rendering
The generator should add an HTML comment in the output like <!-- built at 2026-03-16, X of Y links active --> for debugging
Update src/commands/list.js to show scheduling status for each link: '⏰ from 2026-04-01' or '⏰ until 2026-12-31' or '⏰ 2026-04-01 → 2026-12-31' or nothing if always active
Update src/config.js validateConfig() to validate scheduled_from and scheduled_until as ISO date strings (YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO 8601) if present. Add to validateUrl-style helper: validateDate(str)
Update src/commands/add.js --from and --until flags to set scheduled_from and scheduled_until on the new link object

notes:
[GIA REJECTED] Hook(s) failed: 001-adr-compliance.py. Alignment score 0.8333 below threshold 1.0000
Date comparison uses Date.now() vs Date.parse(scheduled_from). All comparisons in UTC. scheduled_from is inclusive (>=), scheduled_until is inclusive (<=). This task depends on LINKS-002, LINKS-004, and LINKS-005 being completed first.
